name: visca-over-ip

metadata.name: visca-over-ip
metadata.summary: A driver package for the VISCA over IP protocol used by some Sony PTZ cameras

documentation:
# VISCA-IP-Controller

Python code for controlling PTZ cameras using VISCA commands over a local network.

`pip install visca-over-ip`

[Documentation](https://visca-over-ip.readthedocs.io)

## Applications

If you are looking for a graphical program to control a camera, use [VISCA IP Controller GUI](https://github.com/misterhay/VISCA-IP-Controller-GUI). For joystick control use [Visca Joystick](https://github.com/International-Anglican-Church/visca-joystick).

classes.name: visca_over_ip.exceptions.ViscaException
documentation: Raised when the camera doesn't like a message that it received

classes.name: visca_over_ip.exceptions.NoQueryResponse
documentation: Raised when a response cannot be obtained to a query after a number of retries

classes.name: visca_over_ip.caching_camera.CachingCamera
documentation:
Uses caching to improve performance and decrease network traffic.
Will quickly break if multiple controllers are connected to a given camera.

classes.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.__init__
documentation:
---
param ip: the IP address or hostname of the camera you want to talk to.
param port: the port number to use. 52381 is the default for most cameras.
---

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera._send_command
documentation:
Constructs a message based ong the given payload, sends it to the camera,
and blocks until an acknowledge or completion response has been received.
\:param command_hex: The body of the command as a hex string. For example: "00 02" to power on.
\:param query: Set to True if this is a query and not a standard command.

> This affects the message preamble and also ensures that a response will be returned and not None

```
return

The body of the first response to the given command as bytes
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera._receive_response
documentation:
Attempts to receive the response of the most recent command.
Sometimes we don't get the response because this is UDP.
In that case we just increment num_missed_responses and move on.
\:raises ViscaException: if the response is an error and not an acknowledge or completion

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.close_connection
documentation:
Only one camera can be bound to a socket at once.
If you want to connect to another camera which uses the same communication port,
first call this method on the first camera.

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_power
documentation: Powers on or off the camera based on the value of power_state

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.info_display
documentation:
Sets the information display mode of the camera
\:param display_mode: True for on, False for off

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.pantilt
documentation:
Commands the camera to pan and/or tilt.
You must specify both pan_position and tilt_position OR specify neither

```
param pan_speed

-24 to 24 where negative numbers cause a right pan, 0 causes panning to stop,
and positive numbers cause a left pan

param tilt_speed

-24 to 24 where negative numbers cause a downward tilt, 0 causes tilting to stop,
and positive numbers cause an upward tilt.

param pan_position

if specified, the camera will move this distance or go to this absolute position
depending on the value of .
Should be a signed integer where 0 is the center of the range.
The camera will stop panning when numbers reach a high enough magnitude, but will not wrap around.
The pan limits are different for different models of camera and can be tightened by the user.

param tilt_position

if specified, the camera will move this distance or go to this absolute position
depending on the value of .
The camera will stop tilting when number reach a high enough magnitude, but will not wrap around.
The tilt limits are different for different models of camera and can be tightened by the user.

param relative

If set to True, the position will be relative instead of absolute.

raises ViscaException

if invalid values are specified for positions

raises ValueError

if invalid values are specified for speeds
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.pantilt_home
documentation: Moves the camera to the home position

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.pantilt_reset
documentation: Moves the camera to the reset position

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.zoom
documentation:
Zooms out or in at the given speed.

```
param speed

-7 to 7 where positive numbers zoom in, zero stops the zooming, and negative numbers zoom out.
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.zoom_to
documentation:
Zooms to an absolute position

```
param position

0-1, where 1 is zoomed all the way in
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.digital_zoom
documentation:
Sets the digital zoom state of the camera
\:param digital_zoom_state: True for on, False for off

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_focus_mode
documentation:
Sets the focus mode of the camera

```
param mode

One of "auto", "manual", "auto/manual", "one push trigger", or "infinity".
See the manual for an explanation of these modes.
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_autofocus_mode
documentation:
Sets the autofocus mode of the camera
\:param mode: One of "normal", "interval", or "one push trigger".

> See the manual for an explanation of these modes.

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_autofocus_interval
documentation:
Sets the autofocus interval of the camera
:param active_time in seconds, interval_time in seconds.

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.autofocus_sensitivity_low
documentation:
Sets the sensitivity of the autofocus to low
\:param sensitivity_low: True for on, False for off

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.manual_focus
documentation:
Focuses near or far at the given speed.
Set the focus mode to manual before calling this method.

```
param speed

-7 to 7 where positive integers focus near and negative integers focus far
```

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.ir_correction
documentation:
Sets the focus IR correction mode of the camera
\:param value: True for IR correction mode, False for standard mode

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.white_balance_mode
documentation:
Sets the white balance mode of the camera
\:param mode: One of "auto", "indoor", "outdoor", "auto tracing", "manual", "color temperature", "one push", or "one push trigger".

> See the manual for an explanation of these modes.

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_red_gain
documentation:
Sets the red gain of the camera
\:param gain: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_blue_gain
documentation:
Sets the blue gain of the camera
\:param gain: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_white_balance_temperature
documentation:
Sets the white balance temperature of the camera
\:param temperature: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_color_gain
documentation:
Sets the color gain of the camera
\:param color: 'master', 'magenta', 'red', 'yellow', 'green', 'cyan', 'blue'
\:param gain: 0-15; initial value is 4

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_gain
documentation:
Sets the gain of the camera
\:param gain: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.autoexposure_mode
documentation:
Sets the autoexposure mode of the camera
\:param mode: One of "auto", "manual", "shutter priority", "iris priority", or "bright".

> See the manual for an explanation of these modes.

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_shutter
documentation:
Sets the shutter of the camera
\:param shutter: 0-21

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.slow_shutter
documentation:
Sets the slow shutter mode of the camera
\:param mode: True for on, False for off

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_iris
documentation:
Sets the iris of the camera
\:param iris: 0-17

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_brightness
documentation:
Sets the brightness of the camera
\:param brightness: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.backlight
documentation:
Sets the backlight compensation mode of the camera
\:param mode: True for on, False for off
exposure compensation

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.set_aperture
documentation:
Sets the aperture of the camera
\:param aperture: 0-255

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.flip_horizontal
documentation:
Sets the horizontal flip mode of the camera
\:param value: True for horizontal flip mode, False for normal mode

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.flip_vertical
documentation:
Sets the vertical flip (mount) mode of the camera
\:param flip_mode: True for vertical flip mode, False for normal mode

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.flip
documentation:
Sets the horizontal and vertical flip modes of the camera
\:param horizontal: True for horizontal flip mode, False for normal mode
\:param vertical: True for vertical flip mode, False for normal mode

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.defog
documentation:
Sets the defog mode of the camera, not supported on all cameras
\:param value: True for defog mode, False for normal mode
noise reduction 2d
noise reduction 3d

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.save_preset
documentation: Saves many of the camera's settings in one of 16 slots (some cameras even have as many as 255 slots)

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.recall_preset
documentation: Instructs the camera to recall one of the 255 saved presets

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera._zero_padded_bytes_to_int
documentation:
---
param signed: is this a signed integer?
param zero_padded: 'bytes like this: 0x01020304'
return: an integer like this 0x1234
---

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.get_pantilt_position
documentation:
---
return: two signed integers representing the absolute pan and tilt positions respectively
---

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.get_zoom_position
documentation:
---
return: an unsigned integer representing the absolute zoom position
---

methods.name: visca_over_ip.camera.Camera.get_focus_mode
documentation:
---
raises ViscaException: if the response is not 2 or 3
return: either 'auto' or 'manual'
---

Represents a camera that has a VISCA-over-IP interface.
Provides methods to control a camera over that interface.

Only one camera can be connected on a given port at a time.
If you wish to use multiple cameras, you will need to switch between them (use {meth}`close_connection`)
or set them up to use different ports.
